methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuamCore is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alon Cohen that has raised $35M to date. The target operates within a frontier-tech sector comprising 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private companies including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-progression methodology, which uses a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progressions applied to the $35M total raised as a valuation proxy, implies a valuation of $70M. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while 7 same-sector comparables exist, none satisfied the narrower filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers needed to generate a statistically meaningful cohort of three or more datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the wide variance in quantum computing company maturity and technology approaches.

The analysis triangulates to a range of $60M to $80M, with a median point of $70M. This narrow one-method result carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include the small addressable peer set for direct Stage A quantum companies, absence of transaction multiples, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the quantum computing market itself. Any valuation beyond $70M would require either demonstrated technical milestones, revenue traction, or a stronger peer dataset to support.
